landing.heroSubtitle: A production-ready full-stack platform with AI-powered chat, knowledge base retrieval, secure authentication, and a modern dashboard.
landing.featureAiChatDesc: Conversational AI assistant with real-time streaming, conversation history, and tool-calling capabilities.
landing.featureRagDesc: Vector search across your documents with retrieval-augmented generation. Ingest PDFs, DOCX, and more.
landing.featureAuthDesc: JWT-based auth with refresh tokens, OAuth integration, session management, and role-based access control.
landing.featureDashboardDesc: System health monitoring, user management, and collection statistics at a glance.
landing.featureApiDesc: Auto-documented FastAPI backend with OpenAPI specs, versioned endpoints, and async database access.
landing.featureRealtimeDesc: WebSocket-powered streaming for instant AI responses and live updates across the application.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Create your account and start exploring the platform.
landing.footerDesc: Full-stack AI application platform with enterprise-grade features.

footer.tagline: The AI assistant that knows your work.

cookies.title: We use cookies
cookies.description: Essential cookies keep you signed in. Optional ones help us understand how the product is used.
cookies.essentialDesc: Required to keep you signed in. Cannot be disabled.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.summary: The legal agreement between you and {appName}. Plain language where we can; precise where we must.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.summary: What we collect, why, and what you can do about it. We don't train on your data — ever.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.summary: What we store in your browser, why, and how to opt out.

about.title: We build {appName} so other founders don't have to.
about.description: The fastest way to ship a real AI SaaS — auth, billing, RAG, agents, the whole boring stack — without rewiring the same plumbing every time.
about.why1: Every AI side-project starts the same way: a beautiful idea, then four weeks of YAML, three decisions about session storage, and one regretful hand-rolled webhook handler.
about.why2: We open-sourced the plumbing — and kept iterating on it across real production apps — so the next person shipping an AI product can start the day on the actual product.

values.title: Ship the boring parts
values.description: Auth, billing, observability — wired and tested so you can focus on what makes your product different.

values.title: Defaults that age well
values.description: We pick conventions that hold up at scale: typed APIs, repository-service split, async everywhere.

values.title: Open, but opinionated
values.description: MIT-licensed source you can fork freely. Strong defaults so the path of least resistance is also the right one.

values.title: AI as a tool, not a goal
values.description: RAG, agents, streaming — first-class but optional. Build calm products that happen to use AI well.

contact.title: Talk to us.
contact.description: Sales, support, partnerships, or just to say hi — pick the channel that fits, we'll get back to you.
contact.officeNote: Remote-first team. Working hours overlap roughly UTC 08:00–18:00.
contact.formNote: Form tries the backend /api/v1/contact endpoint first. If it's not implemented, it opens your mail client (mailto). Update contact info in

form.fallbackBody: Your mail client should be drafting a message. If nothing happened, email us directly.
form.errorRequired: Please fill out name, email, and message.
form.errorEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
form.policyHint: We reply within 24 hours on weekdays. By sending this you agree to our

help.title: How can we help?
help.description: Browse topics or jump straight to a question. Still stuck? Email support — we reply fast.

topics.title: Getting started
topics.description: Sign up, first chat, connect your data.

items.q: How do I create my first conversation?
items.a: Sign in, click 'New chat' from the dashboard, then type a prompt. The agent will stream the response, including any tool calls it makes along the way.

items.q: Can I import data from Google Drive?
items.a: Yes — under Knowledge bases, add a sync source for Google Drive. You'll authenticate via OAuth and pick a folder. Files re-sync on a schedule you choose.

items.q: Which AI model is used by default?
items.a: We default to a sensible cost/quality balance per provider. You can switch models per-conversation from the model picker in the chat input.

items.q: Why does the assistant pause to ask for approval?
items.a: Some tools (write actions, sending messages, web fetches) can be configured to require human approval. You'll see a confirmation panel inline — approve or reject and the agent resumes.
Hover the most recent assistant message and click the regenerate icon. The agent re-runs the last prompt with the same model and tools.
PDF, DOCX, MD, TXT out of the box. With LlamaParse enabled, 130+ formats including PPT, XLSX, images and more.
Cosine similarity over embeddings. Score thresholds and re-ranking can be configured per collection.
Monthly subscription credits expire at the end of each billing cycle. Pay-as-you-go top-ups never expire.
Each plan includes a base seat count. Add seats from the billing portal — pro-rated and charged immediately.
From the dashboard, click 'Invite team'. Enter emails — they'll get a magic link to join your workspace.

items.q: Where do I generate an API key?
items.a: Settings → API keys. Keys are revealed once on creation; store yours in a secret manager. Revoke anytime.
TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest. Database backups encrypted with KMS-managed keys. See the Security page for details.

items.q: Do you train on my data?
items.a: No. Your prompts and uploaded documents are never used to train any model — ours or third-party.

help.stillStuckBody: Email is the fastest way to get unstuck. We reply within 24 hours on weekdays.

changelog.title: What's new
changelog.description: Releases, improvements, and fixes shipping in {appName}.

community.title: Builders ship faster together.
community.description: A small, opinionated community of devs and founders shipping AI products on top of the template. Async, technical, low-noise.

channels.title: Discord
channels.description: Real-time async chat with other builders. Share what you're shipping, get unstuck.

channels.title: GitHub
channels.description: Source code, issues, discussions. Star to bookmark, file an issue if you hit a bug.

channels.title: Twitter / X
channels.description: Build-in-public updates, release notes, and the occasional technical thread.

channels.title: Office hours
channels.description: Weekly office-hours stream — we walk through real builds and answer questions.

community.guidelinesIntro: We keep the bar high but the rules short. Treat each other well, post in the right place, don't spam.

guidelines.title: Be helpful
guidelines.description: Answer questions when you can. Even pointing someone to the right doc page is meaningful.

guidelines.title: Be specific
guidelines.description: Sharing what you tried beats sharing what you want. Logs, code, screenshots — concrete beats abstract.

guidelines.title: Share wins
guidelines.description: Shipped something? Tell us. We boost cool launches in newsletter and on social.

guidelines.title: No selling
guidelines.description: Don't DM-spam to pitch. Don't post your unrelated startup in the help channels. We mute, we don't ban — usually.

security.title: We treat your data like ours.
security.description: Practical, no-nonsense security posture. We default to the boring choices, document what we do, and patch fast.

pillars.title: Encryption everywhere
pillars.description: TLS 1.2+ in transit, AES-256 at rest. Encrypted backups with KMS-managed keys, rotated quarterly.

pillars.title: Strong auth, by default
pillars.description: Bcrypt password hashing, JWT access + refresh tokens, optional OAuth providers, API keys constant-time-compared.

pillars.title: Least-privilege access
pillars.description: Role-based access control inside the app, scoped database roles in production, audit-logged admin actions.

pillars.title: Tenant isolation
pillars.description: Per-organization data partitioning. Cross-tenant queries are guarded at the repository layer, never trusted at the API.

pillars.title: We don't train on your data
pillars.description: Prompts, documents, and conversations are never used to train any model. Period.

pillars.title: Vulnerability response
pillars.description: Patches for CVEs in dependencies merged within 72 hours. Coordinated disclosure for issues reported by researchers.

checklist: TLS 1.2+ on all traffic; HSTS preload
AES-256 encryption at rest for database and object storage
JWT short-lived access tokens (15 min) + httpOnly refresh tokens
API keys hashed in database; never logged or echoed
Per-org row-level scoping at the repository layer
Stripe handles all card data — we never see PAN/CVV
Incident postmortems published for any user-facing incident
security.vulnBody: Found something? We want to know. Email {email} with steps to reproduce. We'll acknowledge within 48 hours and credit researchers who follow coordinated disclosure.

blog.title: Notes from the workshop.
blog.description: Engineering deep-dives, product decisions, and the occasional rant. Posts every couple of weeks, no marketing fluff.
blog.noPostsDesc: Drop the first MDX into content/blog and rebuild — the index renders automatically.

landing.metaDescription: An AI assistant that knows your team's work. Plug in your docs, your tools, and your data — get answers grounded in everything your team has ever shipped.

hero.description: Plug in your docs, your tools, and your data. Ask anything — get answers grounded in everything your team has ever shipped.

how.title: Three steps to a smarter team

features.title: Built-in features that ship today
features.subtitle: The boring infrastructure parts you'd otherwise spend a quarter wiring up.

pricing.subtitle: Drag the slider — your plan, your price, in real time.

items.a: It's an AI assistant that lives on top of your team's documents, conversations, and tools. Ask it questions in natural language; get answers grounded in your own data, with sources cited.
Your data stays in your workspace. We never train on your content, and we encrypt everything at rest and in transit. Bring your own keys if you need extra control.
Out of the box: Google Drive, Notion, Slack, GitHub, Confluence, and any S3-compatible bucket. More integrations ship every month, and you can always pipe data in via the public API.
Yes — every paid plan is month-to-month and you can cancel from the billing dashboard. Your account stays active until the end of the period you've already paid for.

items.q: Do you offer SSO and audit logs?
items.a: SSO (Google, Okta, Microsoft Entra) and audit logs come with the Business plan. SCIM provisioning and DLP controls are available on Enterprise — get in touch.
Yes. Every feature in the dashboard is also available over a JSON API. Stream chat responses, ingest documents, manage users, subscribe to events — see the developer docs.

finalCta.description: Free 14-day trial. No credit card. Cancel anytime.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.description: Start free. Upgrade when you're ready — every plan includes a 14-day trial.
pricing.demoBanner: Showing template plans — connect Stripe to see your real catalog.

faqs.a: Yes — upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at any time from your billing portal. Changes pro-rate automatically.
Unused monthly credits expire at the end of each billing cycle. Top-up credit packs you purchase separately never expire.

faqs.q: What payment methods do you accept?
faqs.a: All major credit and debit cards via Stripe, plus ACH and SEPA on annual plans. Enterprise can pay via invoice.
Yes. Every paid plan comes with a 14-day free trial — no credit card required to start.
Yes. The template is open-source. Self-host on your own infra, or pay us to manage it.

faqs.q: Do you offer discounts for annual billing?
faqs.a: Annual plans get 2 months free (~17% saving). Toggle the billing switch above to see annual pricing.
